title: Harriet Tubman Tribute Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill says that the Treasury Secretary must put Harriet Tubman's picture on all new twenty dollar bills starting in 2031. If there are serious problems with security or money issues that would make printing the new bills dangerous, the Secretary can wait up to two more years. If the Secretary decides to wait, they have to tell Congress why they are delaying the new bills. This means that by 2033 at the latest, all new twenty dollar bills will have Harriet Tubman's picture on them.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Harriet Tubman Tribute Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Likeness of Harriet Tubman Required to be Included on the Face of Twenty Dollar Federal Reserve Notes
sections.text: This section changes an existing law about money. Starting after December thirty first, two thousand thirty, all new twenty dollar bills must have a picture of Harriet Tubman on the front side. The Secretary of the Treasury is the government official who must make sure this happens. However, the Secretary can delay this requirement for up to two more years if needed. To delay it, the Secretary must first talk with several government agencies that deal with money and security. These agencies are the Bureau of Engraving and Printing which prints money, the Federal Reserve Board which helps manage the money system, and the United States Secret Service which protects against fake money. After talking with these agencies, the Secretary must decide that printing the new bills by the deadline would create serious problems. These problems could be making it too easy for criminals to create fake money, or causing other serious harm to how money works safely and quickly in the United States. If the Secretary decides to delay, they must send a written explanation to two important committees in Congress that oversee banking and money matters.
